test_cases.input_summary: Summarising an epidemiological cohort study on the association between ultra-processed food consumption and cardiovascular disease risk
test_cases.notes: Methodology was accurately classified as observational with clear explanation of why causation cannot be inferred. Effect sizes were reported clearly. Bottom line was measured and accurate.

test_cases.input_summary: Summarising a meta-analysis of interventions to reduce plastic pollution in freshwater ecosystems
test_cases.notes: Good explanation of what a meta-analysis is and its strengths. Heterogeneity between studies was correctly flagged as a limitation. Plain-language translations of statistical concepts were clear.

test_cases.input_summary: Summarising a randomised controlled trial of a new teaching method's effect on student reading scores
test_cases.notes: RCT design was correctly classified and explained. Effect size was contextualised for the education field. Limitations section correctly identified the short follow-up period and the Hawthorne effect risk.
